author: lOrm
content:
## OC current-behavior spec (HEAD ba62e46d) тАФ Phase-2 pin

Describes what `src/tools/web.rs` and `src/web.rs` do TODAY.

## B1 тАФ `execute_web_fetch(args)`: Jina Reader fetch with capped output
**OC location**: `src/tools/web.rs:103-134` (`execute_web_fetch`); `src/web.rs:526-565` (`fetch_url`).
**Contract**: `(args: &HashMap<String, Value>) -> (String, bool)` where `bool` is `is_error`. (a) Typed extract `args.arg_str("url")`; missing/non-string тЖТ `(err_string, true)`. (b) Prefix gate: must start with `"http://"` or `"https://"`; otherwise `("Invalid URL: must start with http:// or https://", true)`. (c) Dispatch via `run_blocking(web::fetch_url(url))` тАФ uses the caller's tokio runtime (`Handle::try_current().ok()` + `block_in_place`) or the process-wide `SHARED_RUNTIME` (`LazyLock<Runtime>`, `multi_thread`, `enable_all`, thread-name `openclaudia-web-tools`). Never builds a fresh runtime per call (fix #368). (d) `fetch_url` runs `validate_url_async` then issues a `GET` to `JINA_READER_URL + url` with `Accept: text/markdown` and `timeout(30s)` via `SHARED_HTTP_CLIENT`. Non-2xx тЖТ `Err(format!("HTTP error: {} - {}", status, url))`. Body is read via `read_bounded_text(response, MAX_WEB_FETCH_BYTES, url)` (fix #745). Title extracted by `content.lines().find(|l| l.starts_with("# "))`. (e) Tool layer formats via `format_fetch_output(title, url, content)` which produces `# {title}\n\nURL: {url}\n\n{content}`, truncating to `MAX_FETCH_OUTPUT_BYTES = 50_000` with a `...\n\n(content truncated, N total chars)` tail.
**Edge cases**: Title absent тЖТ no `# heading` line. Body exactly at cap тЖТ no truncation marker (boundary tested in `format_fetch_output_truncates_at_cap_boundary_807`). `safe_truncate` is UTF-8-boundary safe.
**Error paths**: Returns `(format!("Failed to fetch URL: {e}"), true)` on `fetch_url` Err.

## B2 тАФ `validate_url_async` SSRF guard
**OC location**: `src/web.rs:444-490` (`validate_url_async`), `:377-407` (`validate_url_parts`), `:153` (`DANGEROUS_HOSTNAMES`).
**Contract**: (1) `Url::parse(url_str)` тАФ parse error тЖТ `"Invalid URL: {e}"`. (2) Scheme allowlist: only `http` / `https`; otherwise `"Unsupported URL scheme: {scheme}"`. (3) Host required тАФ `"URL has no host"` if absent. (4) Denylist: `DANGEROUS_HOSTNAMES.iter().any(|h| *h == host_lower)` тЖТ `"URL host '{host}' is a known internal/metadata endpoint"`. (5) IP-literal hosts (decimal, hex, IPv6-brackets) are parsed via `parse_host_as_ip` and run through `validate_resolved_ip` тАФ reserved/internal ranges rejected without DNS. (6) Hostname hosts go through `tokio::net::lookup_host((host, port))` (async, non-blocking); empty тЖТ `"Host '{host}' did not resolve to any address"`. (7) Each resolved `SocketAddr.ip()` is validated; reserved/internal тЖТ reject.
**Edge cases**: `file://`, `data:`, `ftp://` rejected at step 2. `0.0.0.0`, `127.0.0.1`, `169.254.169.254` (metadata) caught either by denylist or reserved-IP check.
**Error paths**: All returns are `Err(String)`. Async version yields the runtime during DNS (fix #673); sync `validate_url` blocks the worker.

## B3 тАФ `execute_web_search(args)`: DDG тЖТ Tavily тЖТ Brave cascade
**OC location**: `src/tools/web.rs:187-232` (`execute_web_search`); `src/web.rs:603-631` (`search_web`).
**Contract**: (a) Typed extract `query`; `query.trim().len() < 2` тЖТ `("Query must be at least 2 characters.", true)`. (b) `limit = args.arg_u64_or("limit", 5) as usize`. (c) `allowed = domain_list("allowed_domains")`, `blocked = domain_list("blocked_domains")` тАФ both lists are accepted simultaneously (no mutual-exclusion check). (d) `config = WebConfig::from_env()`. (e) `run_blocking(web::search_web(query, &config, limit))`. (f) `search_web` cascades: tries `search_duckduckgo(query, limit)` first (headless Chrome, free); on Err logs `warn!` and falls back to Tavily if `TAVILY_API_KEY` set, then Brave if `BRAVE_API_KEY` set. If all unavailable тЖТ composite error string. (g) Tool layer applies domain filters: results with unparseable URLs are KEPT (`host_of` Some-guard). For each parseable URL, `blocked_domains` removes matches; non-empty `allowed_domains` keeps only matches. `domain_matches` covers exact host and subdomain (`host.ends_with(".{needle}")`). (h) Output via `format_search_results(&results)`.
**Edge cases**: `host_of` correctly handles userinfo (`http://user:pass@host`) and IPv6 literals (`[::1]`) via `url::Url` (fix #763). `www.` prefix is stripped before comparison. Blocked takes precedence when both lists name the same domain.
**Error paths**: Search backend Err тЖТ `(format!("Search failed: {e}"), true)`.

## B4 тАФ `execute_web_browser(args)`: headless Chrome fallback
**OC location**: `src/tools/web.rs:236-262`; `src/web.rs:888-939` (`fetch_with_browser` under `#[cfg(feature = "browser")]`), `:947-950` (stub).
**Contract**: (a) Typed extract `url`. (b) Prefix gate identical to `execute_web_fetch`. (c) `fetch_with_browser(url)` (sync, NOT routed through `run_blocking`): calls `validate_url(url)` (sync), launches `Browser::new(LaunchOptions::default_builder().headless(true).build())`, `new_tab()`, `navigate_to(url)`, `wait_until_navigated()`, `std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_secs(2))` for JS render, then `tab.get_content()`. Size-caps via `if content.len() > MAX_WEB_FETCH_BYTES { return Err(...) }` (fix #745). Title via `tab.get_title().ok()`. (d) Returns `format_fetch_output(title, url, content)` тАФ same shape as `execute_web_fetch`. (e) Without `browser` feature flag тЖТ stub returns `("Browser feature not enabled. Rebuild with `cargo build --features browser`", true)`.
**Edge cases**: 2-second sleep is fixed and not configurable. Validation calls SYNC `validate_url` which blocks the calling thread for DNS.
**Error paths**: Any launch/navigation/content-retrieval error тЖТ `(format!("Browser fetch failed: {e}"), true)`.

## B5 тАФ `MAX_FETCH_OUTPUT_BYTES` and `format_fetch_output` shape
**OC location**: `src/tools/web.rs:62` (constant), `:83-100` (`format_fetch_output`).
**Contract**: Single shared formatter тАФ both `execute_web_fetch` and `execute_web_browser` route through it (fix #807). Cap = 50_000 bytes. Layout: optional `# {title}\n\n` then `URL: {url}\n\n` then `content` verbatim. When total > cap, output becomes `{safe_truncate(output, cap)}...\n\n(content truncated, {pre_truncation_total} total chars)`. Pre-truncation total includes the header.
**Edge cases**: Title `None` omits the leading `#` heading entirely (no blank line either). Truncation marker echoes the FULL pre-truncation length, not the cap.
**Invariants**: `safe_truncate` walks back to a UTF-8 char boundary. Pinned by `format_fetch_output_pins_shape_807` and `format_fetch_output_truncates_at_cap_boundary_807`.
